lessons.rule: Do not use 'axios'. Use native 'node:http' or 'fetch' if available. PRD V4 strictly prohibits external HTTP clients to minimize dependencies.
Do not use 'express'. Use 'fastify' for high-performance requirements as per PRD V4.
Before generating any task, confirm that the PRD (Product Requirements Document) is fully loaded and all required sections are defined; otherwise, halt and request the missing PRD content.
Files containing JSX must use the .tsx extension and include the necessary React import (or rely on automatic JSX runtime) to avoid TypeScript syntax errors during compilation.
Always validate and sanitize file paths to prevent directory traversal; reject any path that attempts to escape the project workspace.
Never allow file paths that escape the project root; validate and sanitize all paths to prevent directory traversal.
Always validate and sanitize file paths to prevent directory traversal; reject any path containing '..' or absolute paths outside the project workspace.
Never allow file paths that escape the project root; validate and sanitize paths before writing.
Never allow file paths that escape the project root; validate and sanitize all file paths before writing.

Always validate and sanitize file paths to ensure they remain within the project workspace; reject any path that attempts to traverse outside the root directory (e.g., containing '..' or absolute paths outside the allowed workspace).
Never allow file paths that escape the project workspace; validate and sanitize all paths to prevent directory traversal attacks.
Always run TypeScript type-check (tsc --noEmit) on new or modified files to catch syntax errors before marking a task as complete.
Always run TypeScript type checking (tsc) as part of the build/CI pipeline to catch syntax errors in .ts/.tsx files early, preventing runtime failures due to invalid syntax.
Always run TypeScript compilation (tsc --noEmit) on new or modified TSX/TS files to catch syntax errors before marking a task as done.
Ensure that prompts sent to the Coder agent stay within the model's token limit to avoid empty responses; break down large tasks or trim unnecessary context.
Run TypeScript type-check (tsc --noEmit) on all .ts/.tsx files as a gate before considering a task complete; integrate this check into CI/lint to fail fast on syntax errors.

Always validate TSX/TypeScript syntax with `tsc --noEmit` (or equivalent lint) before committing; fix any syntax errors such as missing parentheses, incorrect JSX, or invalid TypeScript constructs to prevent build failures.

Always ensure the model's response stays within its token limit; if a request risks exceeding the limit (e.g., finish=length with empty stream), truncate the prompt or split the task into smaller calls to avoid empty responses.

Always ensure that generated responses stay within the model's token limit; if a task risks exceeding the limit, break it into smaller subtasks or request a continuation rather than allowing the stream to end with an empty response.
All generated files must be placed under one of the approved project root directories: apps/, packages/, or supabase/. Files outside these directories will be rejected.
Always run TypeScript type checking (tsc --noEmit) on all .ts/.tsx files and resolve any syntax errors before moving forward; treat tsc failures as blocking issues.

All generated source files must be placed within the project's allowed root directories (apps/, packages/, supabase/). Files outside these roots (e.g., under src/) violate the PATH VIOLATION rule and will be rejected.
Always keep LLM responses within the model's token limit; if a task risks exceeding the limit, break it into smaller sub‑tasks or request a continuation rather than allowing the stream to end empty.

When generating test mock files, ensure all imports are resolvable and the file is syntactically valid TypeScript (run `tsc --noEmit` on the mock) before executing the test suite to avoid syntax‑error failures.
Always run TypeScript type checking (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`) on new or modified .ts/.tsx files and resolve any syntax errors before marking a task as complete.

Always validate TypeScript syntax with `tsc --noEmit` on individual files before committing; fix any syntax errors such as missing semicolons, incorrect import/export statements, or invalid JSX to prevent compilation failures.
Keep the context provided to the Coder agent within the model's token limit to prevent empty responses due to length truncation.

Always keep the model's response within its token limit; if a response is approaching the limit, truncate it and continue in a subsequent call or request a higher max_tokens setting to avoid empty streams.
Always validate TypeScript syntax with `tsc --noEmit` (or equivalent linting) before committing changes to prevent build failures due to syntax errors.
Always keep prompts and expected responses within the model's token limit; if a request nears the limit, break the task into smaller subtasks or truncate context to avoid empty stream endings.

Ensure LLM responses stay within the model's token limit; if the response would be truncated, either request a continuation with increased max_tokens or truncate gracefully and ask for the next chunk to avoid empty outputs.
